# Opportunity Idea: Turmeric Market Survey

FLWs visit markets to photograph turmeric vendors, capturing a yellow MTN card
in each photo as a visual reference. Each visit also records the GPS location
of the vendor. The goal is to build a geo-tagged dataset of turmeric market
presence, pricing, and availability across a target area.

## Why it matters

Turmeric sold in many markets is adulterated with lead chromate, a bright
yellow industrial pigment. Lead chromate is highly toxic, particularly for
children. Adulterated turmeric tends to appear shinier and more uniformly
bright than pure turmeric; photos with a standard color reference (the yellow
MTN card) let analysts compare samples across markets and flag candidates for
lab-based follow-up testing.

## Scope

- One stage, one visit per vendor (no follow-up visits)
- Per-vendor: GPS auto-captured, one photo with MTN card visible, short form
- Short vendor-education conversation at the end of each visit
- Max 20 vendor visits per FLW per day; max 5 per market per day

## Deliverables wanted

- Geo-tagged photo dataset across target markets
- Summary table of pricing, form, and stock availability by market
- Map of vendor locations
- Notes on turmeric origin and quality for follow-on analysis
